reasoning: Initial analysis of the FRED data (MORTGAGE15US) indicates positive momentum over the past few weeks (moving from 5.79 to 5.89). A simple linear extrapolation from the recent six months, coupled with seasonal estimation from prior years, yields an aggregated P(increase) of around 69% for the short term. This drives the early April 2025 estimate confidently above 50% (at 61%). However, broader macroeconomic and monetary policy expectations suggest a potential Fed easing cycle could eventually exert downward pressure on yields dragging into late 2025 and 2026. Therefore, the long-term trend becomes much more uncertain. Strictly adhering to the instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon: probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5," I sequence the probabilities downward toward the 50% uninformative prior over time. The estimates sequentially regress to 58% for late April, 55% for June, 53% for September, and finally 51% for March 2026, cleanly mapping the escalating uncertainty as the forecast horizon extends.
